title: Bridge Paper: Exposure to nature is associated with decreased functional connectivity within the distress network: A resting state EEG study.
author: Frontier Science / Hermes T2

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Ecology/Animism** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge thesis:** Nature connectedness — biophilia hypothesis, nature neuroscience, environmental neuroscience.
**Phase context:** nature connectedness, biophilia, nature neuroscience, environmental neuroscience, nature exposure

Paper: Exposure to nature is associated with decreased functional connectivity within the distress network: A resting state EEG study.
DOI: 10.3389/fpsyg.2023.1171215
Abstract: INTRODUCTION: Despite the well-established evidence supporting the restorative potential of nature exposure, the neurophysiological underpinnings of the restorative cognitive/emotional effect of nature are not yet fully understood. The main purpose of the current study was to investigate the association between exposure to nature and electroencephalography (EEG) functional connectivity in the distress network. METHODS: Fifty-three individuals (11 men and 42 women; mean age 21.38 ± 1.54 years) we
